occurrence_sentence: IonQ CEO: Strong AI Will Likely Be Quantum AI, Dammit
Strongly quantum — IonQ CEO talks quantum AI and strong AI.

In an interview with the magazine, IonQ CEO Peter Chapman said that recent work points the way toward quantum computing's ability to foster strong AI, which is the theoretical idea that a computer could replicate the way humans learn and understand.
It's actually one of the reasons I joined IonQ.
Despite his interest in AI, Chapman told the magazine that using AI is up to IonQ's customers, not the company.
Despite his interest in AI, Chapman told the magazine that using AI is up to IonQ's customers, not the company. Building the best quantum computer is the company's goal.

"There's so much to be to be done in a quantum," Chapman said. "From education at one end all the way to the quantum computer itself. I think some of our competitors have taken on lots of the entire problem set. We at IonQ are just focused on producing the world's best quantum computer for them. We think that's a large enough task for a little company like us to handle."

interaction_phrase: Building the best quantum computer is the company's goal
occurrence_sentence: Despite his interest in AI, Chapman told the magazine that using AI is up to IonQ's customers, not the company. Building the best quantum computer is the company's goal. And that keeps the team busy enough.

interaction_phrase: Matt Swayne has served as a writer, editor and analyst at The Quantum Insider since its inception
occurrence_sentence: With a several-decades long background in journalism and communications, Matt Swayne has worked as a science communicator for an R1 university for more than 12 years, specializing in translating high tech and deep tech for the general audience. He has served as a writer, editor and analyst at The Quantum Insider since its inception.
